title: new analyzer: wrong predicates apply order
description:
### Describe what's wrong
Greetings,
Testing new analyzer we caught strange behavior - in some cases performance strongly degrades.
Further research shown, that the reason is in full reading of data, despite predicates.
We have managed to simplify reproduction, real business cases are much more complicated.
The first case: https://fiddle.clickhouse.com/5b2b57a6-8b17-4084-8fe4-2c38bfab367d
In this case direct predicates to fact table, are not used on data reading step and applied after joining
The second case: https://fiddle.clickhouse.com/0ac5a70e-b713-47bf-826a-91b10f764a5b
In this case ALL predicates are applied AFTER JOINS
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
**Which ClickHouse server version to use**
Reproducible in versions:
* ClickHouse 25.9.5.21 (cluster mode, 1shard 3 replicas)
* ClickHouse 25.9.4.58 (cluster mode, 1shard 3 replicas)
* ClickHouse 25.11.3.54 (cluster mode, 1shard 3 replicas)
* https://fiddle.clickhouse.com:latest - 25.11.3.54
**Which interface to use, if matters**
It doesn't matter.
**Non-default settings, if any**
It doesn't matter.
**CREATE TABLE statements for all tables involved**
```sql
CREATE DATABASE temp ON CLUSTER ${CLUSTER};
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp.try_facts_table ON CLUSTER ${CLUSTER};
CREATE TABLE temp.try_facts_table ON CLUSTER ${CLUSTER}
(
data_type 	Nullable(String),
data_date 	Nullable(Date),
col_1  		Nullable(Int32),
col_2  		Nullable(Int32),
col_3  		Nullable(Int32),
col_4  		Nullable(Int32),
json_string Nullable(String)
)
ENGINE = ReplicatedMergeTree
ORDER BY (data_type, data_date)
SETTINGS allow_nullable_key = 1
;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp.try_dict_table_c1 ON CLUSTER ${CLUSTER};
CREATE TABLE temp.try_dict_table_c1 ON CLUSTER ${CLUSTER}
(
col_1  			Nullable(Int32),
col_1_value  	Nullable(String)
)
ENGINE = ReplicatedMergeTree
ORDER BY (col_1)
SETTINGS allow_nullable_key = 1
;
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE temp.try_dict_table_c2 (col_2 INT, col_2_value VARCHAR(255));

INSERT INTO temp.try_dict_table_c2 SELECT MOD(number, 7) AS col_2, CONCAT('value_s_', MOD(number, 7)) AS col_2_value FROM (SELECT 0 AS number UNION ALL SELECT 1 UNION ALL SELECT 2 UNION ALL SELECT 3 UNION ALL SELECT 4 UNION ALL SELECT 5 UNION ALL SELECT 6) AS numbers;
CREATE TABLE temp.try_facts_table (data_type VARCHAR(255), data_date DATE, col_1 INT, col_2 INT, col_3 INT, col_4 INT, json_string TEXT);
key_bug_features: Predicates applied after joins instead of before
